purpose: Defines a pluggable backend integration for record synchronization
usage: Core runtime depends on a sync port while concrete adapters target specific databases or ELNs

purpose: Defines migration order where non-interactive runtime is stabilized before desktop UI integration
usage: Used to sequence migration phases and reduce UI-coupling risk during refactor

purpose: Defines the current-state structural snapshot of layers and major runtime components
usage: Updated when component boundaries or runtime composition materially change

purpose: Defines ownership boundaries for major modules/objects and what they must not own
usage: Used to prevent overlap and maintain clean boundaries during refactor

purpose: Defines a durable record of architectural decisions and tradeoffs
usage: Created for major direction changes and linked from plans/checklists

purpose: Defines enforceable dependency and ownership rules across layers during migration
usage: Updated when dependency policy changes or exceptions are approved

purpose: Defines tests that validate `dpost` scaffolding and cutover behavior
usage: Selected with pytest marker `migration` and focused under `tests/migration`

purpose: Defines delivery order where framework contracts are implemented before concrete integrations
usage: Used to gate migration phases so plugins/adapters follow kernel validation

purpose: Defines core runtime contracts/composition paths independent of concrete integrations
usage: Built and validated before migrating specific plugins or sync adapters

purpose: Defines a minimal concrete implementation used to validate framework contracts
usage: Examples include noop sync adapter and test plugin flow in migration tests

purpose: Defines the application-layer sync contract used by framework composition and orchestrators
usage: Implemented by infrastructure adapters so runtime logic stays backend-agnostic

purpose: Defines a backend-free sync adapter used for framework validation and local headless paths
usage: Selected as default in early dpost migration phases to avoid concrete backend coupling

purpose: Defines the dpost wrapper that delegates sync calls to the legacy Kadi manager with lazy optional dependency loading
usage: Selected with DPOST_SYNC_ADAPTER=kadi and raises startup error when kadi_apy is unavailable

purpose: Defines a named PC and device plugin selection used by dpost kernel composition
usage: Configured with DPOST_PLUGIN_PROFILE for framework validation startup paths
